description: Value-canonical struct keying (§59.4/§59.5). A [Route:int] map is keyed by a struct value. .insert stores under the struct {from:"DAL",to:"HOU"}; the derived .getOr reads with a SYNTACTICALLY DISTINCT struct literal of the same field values and HITS (returns 4500) — proving keys are keyed by VALUE (structural ==), not by reference/identity. Two structurally-equal struct values are the same key (the §59.5 hash-consistency keystone falls out of §45). Observed via impl-neutral derived scalars + DOM.
rationale: §59.4 struct keys 'just work' — structural == (§45.2) is the key-identity; §59.5 two §45-equal values produce byte-identical value-canonical strings, hence the same key. insert then getOr with a distinct-but-equal struct -> 4500, size 1.
